mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household consumption capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal consumption expenditures and a decline in the federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy fueling consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening corporate output capacity and margin expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism yields negative returns in >50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly drop in housing starts reflects collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >65% of quarters following a >10% housing starts decline, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with slowing real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative returns, or forward returns are positive in >55% of stagflationary observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates robust economic momentum without overheating, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion regimes.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 during expansion phases.
